title: Sub-package tests failed with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND — root npm ci doesn't install nested projects
summary: A repo carried an independent npm project in a subdirectory (worker/). After a fresh clone/worktree plus a root `npm ci`, the subproject's test runner crashed with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND because its own node_modules never existed.
mistake: Running `npm ci` at the repository root and assuming every test suite in the tree was now runnable. Without npm workspaces, a nested package.json (worker/, apps/*, functions/) is a separate installation unit — its devDependencies (test pool, CLI tools) are simply absent until installed in place.
instead: After cloning or creating a worktree, list nested manifests first: `find . -name package.json -not -path '*/node_modules/*'`. Run `npm ci` inside each nested project before invoking its scripts. In CI, give each nested project its own install step (and its own cache key / cache-dependency-path).
